business_rationale: Under delivery deadline pressure, splitting a large purchase need into smaller requests may speed up approvals by avoiding higher-level scrutiny, aiming to secure the service faster.
control_concern: Splitting a single purchase need into multiple sub-threshold requests may circumvent approval limits, reducing control effectiveness over large expenditures made under deadline pressure.
expected_effect: This behavior could fragment approval oversight, potentially allowing the cumulative purchase amount to exceed control thresholds without triggering higher-level review.
proposed_behavior: Create multiple separate purchase requests for the same quarter-end analytics service package on the same day, each slightly below the approval threshold.
review_evidence: Multiple purchase requests linked to the same purchase_need_id and vendor_id created on the same request_date.
Individual request amounts just below known or expected approval thresholds.
Short time intervals between these requests.
Absence of consolidated review or flagged aggregation in approval workflows.
